title: 怪屋
summary:
After Sophia encourages her mother, Magda Leonidis, regarding her performance, Sophia signals for the narrator to follow Inspector Taverna out of the room. The narrator asks Taverna about their specific identity and purpose in the house. Taverna advises the narrator not to explain themselves, noting that in such an unstable house, people will likely take their presence for granted. Taverna explains that his questioning is a tactic to assess the inhabitants and gather clues, adding that Margarita Leonidis will reveal much if she is not interrupted, though her credibility is low. Taverna's primary goal is to determine the motive for the cyanide poisoning.

The pair encounters a clumsy, large man who invites them into the living room and leaves to find cigarettes. In the living room, they meet Clementine Leonidis, the wife of Roger Leonidis. Clementine is described as a sharp, alert, and distinctive woman around fifty years old. When Taverna reveals the cause of death was cyanide poisoning, Clementine acknowledges it as murder. She notes that while she has a good relationship with her father-in-law, she dislikes his life goals and methods. She expresses uncertainty regarding a potential affair between Brenda Leonidis and Lawrence Brown.

Roger Leonidis returns after a phone call and reacts with intense anger, accusing a woman of being cold-blooded and suggesting she murdered his father. Roger expresses deep guilt over disappointing his father and declares a desire to strangle the woman responsible. Clementine reprimands him for his outburst, and Roger leaves the room in embarrassment. Clementine dismisses Roger's capability for violence, stating he cannot even swat a fly. During questioning, Clementine provides her whereabouts for the day of the death: Roger was at the London Boxes House catering company before returning home, and Clementine was working at the Lembert Institute on Gour Road until six o'clock.
